Original Article Title: OceanLotus: From external espionage to domestic targeting

Original Article Title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Summary: Attackers compromised over 400 Arch User Repository (AUR) packages by hijacking orphaned projects and modifying build scripts to deploy a Rust-based credential stealer. When executed with root privileges, the malware installs an eBPF rootkit to maintain persistence and exfiltrate data via HTTP.
Source Title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit

Original Article Title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Summary: Attackers compromised over 400 Arch User Repository (AUR) packages by hijacking orphaned projects and modifying build scripts to deploy a Rust-based credential stealer. When executed with root privileges, the malware installs an eBPF rootkit to maintain persistence and exfiltrate data via HTTP.
Source Title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit

Original Article Title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Summary: The 'deps' malware is a Linux credential stealer distributed via a supply-chain compromise of the Arch User Repository (AUR) through a malicious npm package named 'atomic-lockfile'. The malware targets developer environments to exfiltrate secrets and includes eBPF rootkit capabilities, utilizing an encoded onion service for command and control.
Source Title: Preliminary analysis of AUR malware

Original Article Title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
Summary: The threat actor Velvet Ant maintained persistent access to a victim organization by leveraging legacy F5 BIG-IP appliances as internal command and control (C&C) servers. They deployed multiple versions of the PlugX remote access tool, including a version that utilized internal file servers to tunnel traffic and evade detection.
Source Title: In-Depth Analysis: Velvet Ant's Prolonged Cyber Attack on a Large Organization

Original Article Title: Threat Brief: Active Exploitation of PAN-OS CVE-2026-0257
Summary: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 has identified active exploitation of the PAN-OS vulnerability CVE-2026-0257, which allows unauthorized attackers to bypass authentication in GlobalProtect portal and gateway components. While attackers are attempting to initiate VPN connections, no post-access lateral movement or malicious behavior has been observed to date.

Original Article Title: ShinyHunters Targets Education Sector with Oracle PeopleSoft Exploit
Summary: The threat actor UNC6240, known as ShinyHunters, is conducting an extortion campaign targeting Oracle PeopleSoft infrastructure using a zero-day remote code execution vulnerability. The attackers utilize compromised staging servers to deploy MeshCentral agents for command and control, facilitating lateral movement and data theft.

Original Article Title: Seeking Counsel: Ongoing Targeted Campaign Against US Law Firms
Summary: The threat cluster UNC3753 is conducting a financially motivated extortion campaign against US law firms using vishing and social engineering to gain remote access. Attackers pose as IT support to trick victims into installing RMM utilities, which are then used to exfiltrate sensitive data for extortion purposes.

Summary: Cisco Talos utilizes a hypothesis-driven threat hunting approach that combines AI and human judgment to identify adversary behaviors that evade traditional alert-based detection. By correlating telemetry across network and endpoint sources, analysts can confirm malicious activity, such as the KongTuke C2 infrastructure, which would otherwise remain ambiguous.
Source Title: Hypotheses, telemetry, and human judgment: Inside Cisco Talos Threat Hunting

Original Article Title: Impersonation, Click Hijacking, and TDS: Inside a Malware Distribution Ecosystem
Summary: Check Point Research identified a large-scale malware distribution ecosystem that impersonates legitimate open-source and freeware projects to capture search traffic. The operation utilizes a sophisticated Traffic Distribution System (TDS) with strict gating mechanisms to deliver various payloads, including infostealers and crypto clippers, to targeted users.

Original Article Title: Argamal: Malware hidden in hentai games

Original Article Title: Pirates in the crosshairs: how one cybercrime gang has been infecting book, movie, and TV show fans for years

The campaign utilizes a rotating infrastructure of domains that resolve to a single C2 IP address to facilitate command and control communications.

Original Article Title: Don't Fear the Repo: UNK_DeadDrop Phishing Campaign Targets Developers to Steal Cryptocurrency
Summary: The UNK_DeadDrop phishing campaign targets developers by masquerading malicious GitHub repositories as technical assignments or cryptocurrency projects. Once cloned and opened in an IDE, the repositories execute platform-specific loaders that install malicious VS Code extensions to enable remote command execution, credential theft, and cryptocurrency wallet exfiltration.

Source Title: Don't Fear the Repo: UNK_DeadDrop Phishing Campaign Targets Developers to Steal Cryptocurrency

Original Article Title: From Fake Purchase Orders to Remote Access: Analyzing the JS.MonoGlyphRAT Threat to US Enterprises
Summary: JS.MonoGlyphRAT is an advanced backdoor delivered via malicious JavaScript files disguised as legitimate business documents like purchase orders. Once executed, the malware establishes persistence, communicates with a C2 server via HTTP, and functions as a loader to deliver additional malicious payloads.
Source Title: From Fake Purchase Orders to Remote Access: Analyzing the JS.MonoGlyphRAT Threat to US Enterprises

Original Article Title: Alert: Exploitation of CVE-2026-34197 in Apache ActiveMQ
Summary: The China-nexus APT group SLIME88 is actively exploiting a critical remote code execution vulnerability, CVE-2026-34197, in Apache ActiveMQ. Attackers use crafted HTTP requests to the Jolokia API to force the broker to fetch malicious XML configurations from a C2 server, subsequently deploying the SoxAgent RAT to compromise Linux devices.
Source Title: Alert: Exploitation of CVE-2026-34197 in Apache ActiveMQ

Original Article Title: Check Point VPN のゼロデイ脆弱性 CVE-2026-50751：ランサムウェア攻撃を確認

Attackers are leveraging this flaw to establish unauthorized VPN sessions and download malicious ELF binaries from controlled infrastructure.

Original Article Title: Linux カーネルの新たな脆弱性 CVE-2026-23111 が FIX：root 権限昇格エクスプロイトが公開
Summary: A use-after-free vulnerability in the Linux kernel's nftables subsystem, tracked as CVE-2026-23111, allows local attackers to escalate privileges to root. A functional exploit has been released that achieves high success rates by manipulating transaction batches to hijack control flow and bypass namespace isolation.
Source Title: Linux カーネルの新たな脆弱性 CVE-2026-23111 が FIX：root 権限昇格エクスプロイトが公開

Original Article Title: Football Fever Fuels Scam Campaigns Across Email and Social Media
Summary: Cybercriminals are exploiting the excitement surrounding the FIFA World Cup 2026 and general football fandom to launch widespread scam campaigns. These operations utilize malvertising on social media, fake online stores, fraudulent mobile apps, and phishing emails to target fans globally with merchandise scams and lottery fraud.
Source Title: Football Fever Fuels Scam Campaigns Across Email and Social Media

These campaigns utilize multi-stage, fileless execution chains and social engineering tactics, such as fake software downloads and ClickFix lures, to deploy various threats including commodity stealers and backdoors.

Original Article Title: From Fake Amazon Security Alert to HarborWatch Agent: ClickFix Delivery of a Custom Monitoring RAT
Summary: Threat actors are conducting an Amazon-themed phishing campaign that utilizes ClickFix social engineering to trick users into executing malicious commands. This process leads to the installation of the HarborWatch Agent, a custom remote access trojan that exfiltrates system information to a C2 server branded as Harbor Sentinel.

This process leads to the installation of the HarborWatch Agent, a custom remote access trojan that exfiltrates system information to a C2 server branded as Harbor Sentinel.
Source Title: From Fake Amazon Security Alert to HarborWatch Agent: ClickFix Delivery of a Custom Monitoring RAT

Original Article Title: Embedded Threats: How Attackers Weaponize Legitimate Emails
Summary: Threat actors are weaponizing legitimate online services by embedding malicious content into arbitrary text fields, such as usernames or meeting descriptions. These malicious inputs are then included in legitimate automated emails, allowing attackers to bypass traditional security filters by leveraging trusted sender addresses.
Source Title: Embedded Threats: How Attackers Weaponize Legitimate Emails

Original Article Title: Borrowed Trust – Systematic Exploitation of Abandoned Cloud DNS Delegations to serve Thai Gambling SEO Content

Original Article Title: OverlayPhantom: The Android Banking Trojan Hiding in Plain Sight
Summary: OverlayPhantom is a sophisticated Android banking trojan that masquerades as legitimate government applications to harvest credentials. It utilizes Accessibility Service permissions to perform overlay attacks, real-time screen streaming, and exfiltrate data to a multi-port command and control infrastructure.
Source Title: OverlayPhantom: The Android Banking Trojan Hiding in Plain Sight

Original Article Title: Chinese APT Campaign Targets Entities with Updated FDMTP Backdoor
Summary: Darktrace researchers identified a Twill Typhoon-linked campaign targeting APJ entities using an updated FDMTP backdoor. The attackers employ CDN impersonation, legitimate binary sideloading, and a modular .NET RAT to maintain persistence and command-and-control communication.

Original Article Title: Shai-Hulud Campaign Evolution: Miasma, Hades, and AI Scanner Evasion

Summary: Google has released a stable channel update for Chrome, addressing 151 security vulnerabilities across various components including GPU, Network, Dawn, WebGL, and ANGLE. Source Title: Chrome Releases: Stable Channel Update for Desktop

Original Article Title: Weekly Report 2026-05-20号
Summary: A vulnerability in the peering authentication mechanism of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager allows unauthenticated, remote attackers to bypass authentication. By sending crafted requests, an attacker can gain administrative privileges and manipulate the SD-WAN fabric configuration via NETCONF.
Source Title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Authentication Bypass Vulnerability

Original Article Title: ランサムウェア攻撃グループ「The Gentlemen」の内部チャットログから見えた攻撃の実態
Summary: The Gentlemen threat group employs various tactics, including the use of Chisel for encrypted SOCKS proxy C2 communication and the abuse of legitimate tools like AnyDesk for persistence. They utilize custom malware and automated scripts to disable security features and facilitate lateral movement within compromised networks.
Source Title: Hastalamuerte Exposed: Inside the Tactics and Procedures of The Gentlemen
